originalBook: On the Latter Part of Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount, Contained in the Sixth and Seventh Chapters of Matthew.
title: Chapter XIV

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: Then, further, the statement which follows, “No man can serve two masters,” is to be referred to this very intent, as He goes on to explain, saying: “For either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will submit to the one, and despise the other.”

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: And these words are to be carefully considered; for who the two masters are he forthwith shows, when He says, “Ye cannot serve God and mammon.”

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: Riches are said to be called mammon among the Hebrews.

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: The Punic name also corresponds: for gain is called mammon in Punic.

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: But he who serves mammon certainly serves him who, as being set over those earthly things in virtue of his perversity, is called by our Lord the prince of this world.

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: A man will therefore “either hate” this one, “and love the other,” i.e. God; “or he will submit to the one, and despise the other.”

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: For whoever serves mammon submits to a hard and ruinous master: for, being entangled by his own lust, he becomes a subject of the devil, and he does not love him; for who is there who loves the devil?

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: But yet he submits to him; as in any large house he who is connected with another man’s maid servant submits to hard bondage on account of his passion. even though he does not love him whose maid-servant he loves.

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: But “he will despise the other,” He has said; not, he will hate.

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: For almost no one’s conscience can hate God; but he despises, i.e. he does not fear Him, as if feeling himself secure in consideration of His goodness.

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: From this carelessness and ruinous security the Holy Spirit recalls us, when He says by the prophet, “My son, do not add sin upon sin, and say, The mercy of God is great ;” and, “Knowest thou not that the patience of God inviteth thee to repentance?”

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: For whose mercy can be mentioned as being so great as His, who pardons all the sins of those who return, and makes the wild olive a partaker of the fatness of the olive? and whose severity as being so great as His, who spared not the natural branches, but broke them off because of unbelief?

verses.book: Our Lord’s Sermon on the Mount.
verses.text: But let not any one who wishes to love God, and to beware of offending Him, suppose that he can serve two masters; and let him disentangle the upright intention of his heart from all doubleness: for thus he will think of the Lord with a good heart, and in simplicity of heart will seek Him.
